description: A solar project financier needs a Monte Carlo risk analysis for a 10 MW utility-scale solar plant in Phoenix, AZ to calculate the P50 and P90 Levelized Cost of Energy (LCOE) under uncertain equipment costs and system losses. Develop and run a Python script at /home/ga/Documents/SAM_Projects/monte_carlo_lcoe.py that performs a 500-iteration Monte Carlo simulation using PySAM. The simulation must use a random seed of 42. Sample 'System Losses (%)' from a Uniform distribution (10.0 to 20.0) and 'Capital Cost ($/kW)' from a Normal distribution (mean 1100.0, std dev 150.0). For each iteration, use the PySAM Pvwattsv8 module for a 10 MW system (tilt: 20, azimuth: 180, DC/AC ratio: 1.2) using the weather file directory specified in /home/ga/.SAM/solar_resource_dir.txt. Calculate LCOE for each iteration using the formula: LCOE = (Capital Cost per kW * 10000 * 0.075 + 150000) / Annual_Energy_kWh. Save the 50th (P50) and 90th (P90) percentiles of the LCOE values to /home/ga/Documents/SAM_Projects/monte_carlo_results.json using the keys "iterations", "p50_lcoe_usd_per_kwh", and "p90_lcoe_usd_per_kwh".
